judge.validator_python:
def validate(args, output):
    s = args[0]
    if not isinstance(output, list):
        return False
    def is_valid(t):
        depth = 0
        for ch in t:
            if ch == '(': depth += 1
            elif ch == ')':
                depth -= 1
                if depth < 0: return False
        return depth == 0
    def min_removals(t):
        left = right = 0
        for ch in t:
            if ch == '(': left += 1
            elif ch == ')':
                if left > 0: left -= 1
                else: right += 1
        return left + right
    min_rem = min_removals(s)
    correct = set()
    from itertools import combinations
    n = len(s)
    for mask_size in range(n + 1):
        removed = mask_size
        if removed > min_rem: break
        if removed < min_rem: continue
        # try removing exactly mask_size characters
        for indices in combinations(range(n), mask_size):
            candidate = ''.join(s[i] for i in range(n) if i not in indices)
            if is_valid(candidate):
                correct.add(candidate)
    seen = set()
    for item in output:
        if not isinstance(item, str): return False
        if item in seen: return False
        seen.add(item)
        if not is_valid(item): return False
        if len(item) != len(s) - min_rem: return False
    return seen == correct

pattern: BFS level by level removes one invalid parenthesis at a time, collecting all valid strings at the first level where any valid string appears, which guarantees the minimum removal count.
hints: Try every possible single removal at each BFS level; if any result is a valid parenthesization, collect all valid ones at that level and stop.
To avoid re-exploring the same string, keep a visited set across BFS levels.
A valid parenthesization has equal open and close counts and no prefix with more closes than opens; checking this in O(n) prunes most candidates quickly.

edge_inputs.description: one extra close at the boundary yields two solutions
letter between parentheses with extra close
open and close with nothing else invalid
all opens need matching closes removed
letters only, always valid as is
mixed letters and multiple invalid parens

stress.description: short string of parentheses and letters up to length 12
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(max(1, size), 12)
    chars = [rng.choice('()()ab') for _ in range(n)]
    return [''.join(chars)]
stress.note: cap at 12 to bound BFS branching; parentheses weighted heavier
